# GENERATED FILE — do not edit by hand.
# Regenerate with: uv run python scripts/gen_llms_txt.py
# Source of truth: the live MCP registry (register_tools() -> mcp.list_tools()/...).
# Unlike a hand-maintained llms.txt, this is regenerated from the registry so it can never
# drift from the actual tool/prompt/resource surface.


# inkscape-mcp — LLM index (llms.txt)

inkscape-mcp — an MCP server that makes Inkscape/SVG documents agent-ready: inspect, edit safely (reversible), validate, render, and export.

Surface: 99 tools, 7 prompts, 16 resources.

## Run & transport
Transport: STDIO only (local). No HTTP.
Run: uv run inkscape-mcp
Console script: inkscape-mcp = inkscape_mcp.server:main

## Environment / configuration
Workspace roots (required): INKSCAPE_MCP_WORKSPACE_ROOTS (OS-path-separator-delimited list of allowed dirs; the sandbox boundary).
Operator-tunable limits / gates (all optional, env-overridable):
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ACTION_ALLOWLIST
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ARTIFACT_KEEP_DAYS
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ARTIFACT_MAX_BYTES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ARTIFACT_MAX_BYTES_PER_DOC
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ENGINE_IDLE_TIMEOUT_S
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ENGINE_MAX_PROCESSES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_ENGINE_MODE
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_EXTENSION_ALLOWLIST
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_LIVE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_LIVE_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_LIVE_COALESCE_BUDGET_MS
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_LIVE_ENABLED
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_LIVE_FRAME_KEEP_DAYS
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_LIVE_FRAME_MAX_BYTES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_MAX_EXPORT_PX
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_MAX_INPUT_BYTES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_MAX_PROCS
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_PROCESS_TIMEOUT_S
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_RAW_ACTION_ENABLED
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_SNAPSHOT_HARD_MAX_BYTES
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_SNAPSHOT_HARD_MAX_N
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_SNAPSHOT_KEEP_DAYS
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_SNAPSHOT_KEEP_N
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_TOOL_DESC
  - INKSCAPE_MCP_TOOL_PROFILE

## Tools (one line + risk class, grouped by category)

### Create
- add_linear_gradient [risk: medium] — Add a `<linearGradient>` to the document `<defs>` (created if absent).
- add_radial_gradient [risk: medium] — Add a `<radialGradient>` to the document `<defs>` (created if absent).
- compose_grid [risk: medium] — Lay out N DIFFERENT assets in a grid (contact / spec sheet) in ONE reversible call.
- create_circle [risk: medium] — Create a `<circle>` centred at `(cx, cy)` with radius `r` (> 0).
- create_document [risk: medium] — Create a blank, tracked working-copy document from scratch — NO source file required.
- create_ellipse [risk: medium] — Create an `<ellipse>` centred at `(cx, cy)` with radii `rx` / `ry` (> 0).
- create_group [risk: medium] — Create an empty `<g>` group inside `parent_id` (must exist) or the document default parent.
- create_line [risk: medium] — Create a `<line>` from `(x1, y1)` to `(x2, y2)`.
- create_path [risk: medium] — Create a `<path>` with the validated `d` data string.
- create_polygon [risk: medium] — Create a closed `<polygon>` from `points` (≥ 1 `(x, y)` pairs).
- create_polyline [risk: medium] — Create an open `<polyline>` from `points` (≥ 1 `(x, y)` pairs).
- create_rect [risk: medium] — Create a `<rect>` at `(x, y)` sized `width` / `height` (> 0), with optional corner radii.
- create_text [risk: medium] — Create a `<text>` element anchored at `(x, y)` holding `text`.
- create_use [risk: medium] — Create a `<use href="#href_id">` referencing an existing same-document object.
- group_objects [risk: medium] — Wrap existing objects (`object_ids`, ≥ 1, all must exist) in a NEW `<g>`.
- insert_svg_fragment [risk: high] — Insert an agent-composed SVG fragment under a parent (`parent_id`) or the document root.
- inspect_document [risk: low] — Inspect a loaded document: tree, layers, styles, fonts, external assets.
- open_document [risk: low] — Open an SVG into a tracked workspace document and return its id + summary.
- place_document [risk: medium] — Place an existing document or object INTO another document at `(x, y)` with `scale`.
- reload_document [risk: low] — Refresh a working copy FROM ITS SOURCE under the SAME `doc_id`, discarding working edits.
- reparent_object [risk: medium] — Move an object (`object_id`) under a new parent (`new_parent_id`); both must exist.
- set_document_svg [risk: high] — REPLACE the whole working copy with an agent-composed SVG string (root must be `<svg>`).

### Inspect
- find_objects [risk: low] — Find addressable object ids in a tracked document by tag / paint / text / id-prefix / bbox.
- quality_report [risk: low] — Build a machine-readable quality report for a document: validation findings plus metrics.
- quality_report_set [risk: low] — Quality-report a SET of documents in one call: per-doc reports + aggregate + verdict.
- validate_document [risk: low] — Validate a loaded document and return structured, machine-readable findings.

### Edit
- apply_edits [risk: medium] — Apply an ordered list of typed DOM edits to a document as ONE atomic, reversible operation.
- apply_palette [risk: medium] — Apply many `from -> to` colour replacements in a single reversible operation.
- delete_object [risk: high] — Delete objects by id from a document in ONE reversible, snapshot-backed operation.
- duplicate_object [risk: medium] — Duplicate an object or group in place, inserting the clone right after the original.
- fit_to_content [risk: medium] — Fit the document's root ``viewBox`` to its CONTENT bounding box.
- move_object [risk: medium] — Translate an object/group by ``(dx, dy)`` in its parent coordinate space.
- normalize_viewbox [risk: medium] — Normalize or repair the document's root ``viewBox``.
- rename_object [risk: medium] — Change an object's `id` and/or its `inkscape:label`.
- replace_color [risk: medium] — Replace one colour with another across the document (or within `scope_ids` subtrees).
- replace_text [risk: medium] — Replace the text content of a text element (`<text>` / `<tspan>` / flow text).
- resize_canvas [risk: medium] — Set the document canvas ``width`` / ``height`` to validated CSS lengths.
- rotate_object [risk: medium] — Rotate an object/group by ``degrees`` about a point.
- scale_object [risk: medium] — Scale an object/group by factor ``sx`` (and ``sy``, defaulting to ``sx`` for uniform).
- set_fill [risk: medium] — Set the fill colour (and optional fill opacity) of one or more objects.
- set_font [risk: medium] — Set font-family / font-size / font-weight on one or more text objects.
- set_opacity [risk: medium] — Set the element-level opacity of one or more objects.
- set_stroke [risk: medium] — Set the stroke colour, width, and/or opacity of one or more objects.
- tile [risk: medium] — Lay out a ``rows`` x ``cols`` grid of an object in ONE reversible operation.

### Paths
- boolean_difference [risk: high] — Subtract the top path(s) from the bottom one via the Inkscape engine (``path-difference``).
- boolean_union [risk: high] — Union two or more paths into one via the Inkscape engine (``path-union``).
- break_apart [risk: high] — Break a compound path into its subpaths via the Inkscape engine (``path-break-apart``).
- cleanup_paths [risk: high] — Reduce path nodes via the Inkscape engine (``path-simplify``) — an explicit alias of
- combine_paths [risk: high] — Combine paths into a single multi-subpath path via the Inkscape engine (``path-combine``).
- simplify_path [risk: high] — Simplify the given path(s) via the Inkscape engine (``path-simplify``).
- stroke_to_path [risk: high] — Convert each object's stroke into a filled path via the Inkscape engine.

### Export
- capture_frame [risk: low] — Capture the next numbered PNG screenshot in a per-run frame series.
- create_icon_set [risk: low] — Export a multi-size square PNG icon set from the source document.
- export_batch [risk: low] — Run a bounded batch of typed export specs in one call (dry-run by default).
- export_document [risk: low] — Export the whole document to PNG, PDF, or SVG.
- export_object [risk: low] — Export a single object (by id) to PNG, PDF, or SVG.
- export_print_profile [risk: low] — Export a print-oriented PDF (vector, page area) of the whole document.
- export_set [risk: low] — Batch-export a SET of documents in one call: per-doc results + aggregate + verdict.
- export_web_profile [risk: low] — Export a web-oriented asset set: a responsive PNG set plus one plain SVG.
- list_frames [risk: low] — List the frames of a `capture_frame` series, ordered by index.
- render_preview [risk: low] — Render a PNG preview of the whole document into the artifacts dir.

### Optimize
- optimize_set [risk: medium] — Web-optimize a SET of documents in one call: per-doc results + aggregate + verdict.
- svg_web_optimize [risk: medium] — Web-optimize an SVG: strip editor metadata, drop dead structure, reduce coordinate precision.

### Live
- check_live_support [risk: low] — Report which live transports are available on this host (read-only; no connection).
- live_apply_to_selection [risk: high] — Apply a validated style and/or simple transform to the current live selection.
- live_arm_socket [risk: restricted] — Auto-arm the extension-socket helper so a programmatic launch gets the FULL live surface.
- live_connect [risk: medium] — Connect to a running Inkscape over the best-ranked available transport (enables live).
- live_diff_view [risk: low] — Produce a FOCUSED, annotated before/after visual diff of a live operation.
- live_disconnect [risk: low] — Disconnect the current live session (the X1 disable switch). Idempotent.
- live_export_selection [risk: low] — Export just the current live selection to a PNG under the live artifacts dir.
- live_get_active_document [risk: low] — Identify the document open in the connected live instance (read-only).
- live_get_scene [risk: low] — Capture one live frame as a PNG PLUS a structured, machine-readable `LiveScene`.
- live_get_selection [risk: low] — Read the current selection in the live instance as object ids (read-only).
- live_insert_svg [risk: high] — Insert an SVG fragment into the running document.
- live_inspect_selection [risk: low] — Inspect the selected objects in the live instance (semantic, by id; read-only).
- live_install_helper [risk: restricted] — Install the shipped extension-socket helper into the Inkscape user extensions dir.
- live_render_view [risk: low] — Rasterize the live canvas to a PNG in the live artifacts dir (visual feedback).
- live_session_step [risk: high] — Run ONE perceive→decide→act→observe iteration of the live-view loop.
- live_set_selected_text [risk: high] — Replace the selected text object's content in the running document.
- live_set_viewport [risk: low] — Control the live canvas viewport: zoom / pan / fit-to-selection / fit-to-page.
- live_status [risk: low] — Report live-session state: enabled, connected, active transport, available transports.
- live_sync_to_workspace [risk: medium] — Save the live document's current state into the workspace as a NEW tracked document.
- live_wait_for_change [risk: low] — Block until the live state changes, or the bounded timeout elapses (read-only).

### Snapshots
- create_snapshot [risk: low] — Snapshot the current working copy of a document and index it.
- list_snapshots [risk: low] — List a document's snapshots in order, with metadata.
- prune_snapshots [risk: low] — Apply the snapshot + live-frame retention policy, pruning superseded server state.
- restore_snapshot [risk: medium] — Revert a document's working copy to a chosen snapshot.

### Discover
- how_do_i [risk: low] — Map a natural-language goal to the concrete inkscape-mcp tool(s) that achieve it.

### System
- diagnose_runtime [risk: low] — Probe the local Inkscape + Python runtime fresh and return the capability matrix.
- discover_extensions [risk: low] — List the server-side allowlisted Inkscape extensions + probe notes (read-only, diagnostic).
- list_actions [risk: low] — Discover the host's actual Inkscape Action surface (probe-driven, version-aware).
- list_capabilities [risk: low] — Return the cached runtime capability matrix (probed once, then reused).
- run_action_chain [risk: high] — Execute a validated Action chain over a document's working copy (HIGH risk, approval-gated).
- run_raw_action [risk: high] — Run ONE raw, allowlisted Inkscape Action over a document (advanced mode; OFF by default).
- save_document_as [risk: medium] — Save a document's current working-copy state to a NEW file in the workspace.
- stat_artifact [risk: low] — Return the on-disk byte size + sha256 digest of one sandboxed artifact.
- stat_artifacts [risk: low] — Stat a SET of sandboxed artifacts: per-file size + sha256 plus an aggregate byte total.
- transform_objects [risk: medium] — Apply ONE typed op to EVERY object a selector matches — one atomic, reversible operation.
- validate_action_chain [risk: low] — Dry-run-validate an ordered Action chain and return its resolved plan (NO mutation).
